questions.question: What is the time complexity of binary search on a sorted array?
questions.explanation: Binary search halves the search space at each step, giving O(log n) time complexity — much faster than O(n) linear search.

questions.question: Which data structure uses LIFO (Last-In, First-Out) ordering?
questions.explanation: A Stack uses LIFO — the last element pushed is the first to be popped. Think of a stack of plates.

questions.question: What is the worst-case time complexity for finding an element in an unsorted Linked List?
questions.explanation: In the worst case, you must traverse all n nodes to find the element or confirm it doesn't exist.

questions.question: Which operation is O(1) for a Linked List but O(n) for an Array (when shifting is required)?
questions.explanation: Inserting at the head of a Linked List is O(1) — just update the head pointer. Arrays require shifting all elements rightward, costing O(n).
